_comment: Generated by macro-analyst on 2026-04-28 (pre-open, Bucharest 07:36 EEST). EUR/RON cooling: BNR Apr 27 ref 5.0927; Yahoo intraday today 5.0866 — testing the 5.05 boundary downward for the first time in 35 sessions. DAX gave back 1.4% intraday, weekly -2.3% — risk tone heavy. Brent giving back the geopolitical premium (-2.6% intraday from $105.33 to $102.63). Coalition crisis active: PSD+AUR file no-confidence motion, AUR aiming May 5 submission. PNRR €10bn / €6bn-at-risk plus 9 reform laws by August. NBR meets May 7 (9 days). Source feeds: BNR XML (FX), Yahoo (^GDAXI, ^VIX, BZ=F, TTF=F, EURRON=X, WIG20.WA), tradingeconomics (RO 10Y), WebSearch for fiscal/political/PNRR/rating.

_qualitative_context.note: Coalition crisis intensifies into Day 8: PSD + AUR formally announced joint no-confidence motion on Apr 27, with AUR aiming to submit May 5 and a vote shortly after. Bolojan refuses to step down; coalition has lost majority. PM still has presidential backing (Nicusor Dan consultations ongoing). Government must pass 9 PNRR-tied reform laws (public-sector wages, fiscal, energy) by August or lose up to EUR 6bn of EUR 10bn PNRR tranche; 57% of remaining critical reforms sit under PSD-coordinated ministries. BET reaction prior session: -1.51% Apr 22, +1.33% Apr 23, +1.12% Apr 24 close 29,289.67 — only -1.1% from ATH (29,615). Market still discounts continuity. Today's risk: vote-of-no-confidence noise plus fiscal-package leak risk. EUR/RON 5.0927 — testing 5.05 boundary downward for first time in 35 sessions, but no break yet (FX-3 not fired). RO 10Y 7.21% — below 7.40% trigger. Brent -2.6% intraday gives up some Iran/MidEast premium, weekly +5.1% still net SNP tailwind. NBR May 7 meeting — first cut consensus but unconfirmed (RAT-3 not yet armed). DAX -1.36% intraday plus VIX 18 → INT-1 marginal. Active themes: NBR pivot (slightly headwind today on vote noise pre-meeting), MSCI upgrade June review (still on), Rating tail (warning level — coalition fall raises downgrade probability, watch agency-action queue), Post-election relief (degrading on coalition collapse). Regime caveat in force: 70% YoY rally to ATH biases mean-reversion; lean toward defensive, skeptical of breakout setups.
_qualitative_context.regime_caveat: 70% YoY rally to ATH still active per reference doc — bias mean-reversion, skeptical of breakouts, weight relief-rally triggers.
